API_GATEWAY_VULNERABLE.title: AWS API Gateway endpoint lacks authorization
API_GATEWAY_VULNERABLE.description: API Gateway routes are configured to receive traffic directly from the internet without requiring authorization tokens or custom authorizers.
API_GATEWAY_VULNERABLE.impact: Exposes core backend Lambdas and services to unauthorized callers, leading to billing overruns, data scraping, or logic bypass.
remediation_steps: Configure a Cognito or JWT custom authorizer on the API Gateway route.
Ensure authorization = "COGNITO_USER_POOLS" or similar is set in the terraform route resource.

IAM_PERMISSIVE_POLICY.title: Ensure IAM policy does not allow wildcard permissions
IAM_PERMISSIVE_POLICY.description: An IAM role or policy definition contains Action or Resource lists with the wildcard '*' character, allowing unrestricted cloud actions.
IAM_PERMISSIVE_POLICY.impact: Violates the principle of least privilege, allowing users or hijacked services to execute admin-level changes or access arbitrary storage resources.
remediation_steps: Limit actions to explicit services and actions (e.g. s3:GetObject instead of s3:*).
Specify exact resource ARNs (e.g. arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket) rather than '*'.
